Include keywords that help agents identify relevant tasks.\n+description: \"Use for UX/UI work on landing pages, dashboards, navigation systems, component styling, layout principles, design tokens, responsive behavior, accessibility, microcopy, and interaction flow improvements.\"\n ---\n \n <!-- Tip: Use /create-skill in chat to generate content with agent assistance -->\n \n-Define the functionality provided by this skill, including detailed instructions and examples\n+# UX Skill\n+\n+This skill provides the default UX workflow, visual language, and implementation guardrails for this workspace.\n+\n+Use it when the task is about:\n+- Designing or refining a landing page, dashboard, list view, or detail page\n+- Choosing layout structure, spacing, typography, colors, and motion\n+- Improving usability, navigation, breadcrumbs, filters, tables, or call-to-action hierarchy\n+- Building non-standard UI that should not look like a default Bootstrap template\n+- Applying the workspace design tokens consistently\n+\n+## When To Use\n+Use this skill for any prompt that mentions:\n+- UX, UI, usability, interaction design, visual hierarchy, accessibility, IA, microcopy\n+- landing page, dashboard, navigation, breadcrumb, list view, detail page, card, table, chart\n+- design system, design tokens, responsive layout, custom styling, polished UI\n+\n+If the request is not UX-related, do not force this skill. Use the default agent instead.\n+\n+## UX Direction\n+The preferred style is a professional operations dashboard with a custom, non-generic feel.\n+\n+### Visual Tone\n+- Dark navigation shell and light content canvas\n+- Confident contrast and clear data hierarchy\n+- Crisp, information-dense sections with generous spacing\n+- Practical, operational, and slightly premium\n+\n+### Color System\n+Use these colors as the default palette:\n+- Sidebar / navigation: `#142638`\n+- App background: `#F4F7FB`\n+- Surfaces / cards: `#FFFFFF`\n+- Border / separators: `#E6ECF2`\n+- Primary accent: `#27B1E6`\n+- KPI yellow: `#F0C419`\n+- KPI violet: `#6E62C6`\n+- KPI green: `#22B455`\n+- KPI sky: `#2EA9DF`\n+- Headings: `#1D2A3A`\n+- Body text: `#4A5A6A`\n+- Muted text: `#7E8B98`\n+\n+### Typography\n+- Prefer a modern geometric sans stack such as `Manrope`, `Plus Jakarta Sans`, or `Segoe UI`\n+- Use strong hierarchy: 700 for primary headings, 600 for section titles, 500 for labels, 400 for body text\n+- Keep headings tight and content readable\n+\n+### Layout Principles\n+- Use a dashboard grid with clear zones: navigation, utilities, filters, KPIs, analytics, detail content\n+- Prioritize progressive disclosure: summary first, detail second\n+- Keep an 8px spacing rhythm\n+- Make desktop the primary layout, then adapt to stacked mobile behavior\n+- Collapse navigation on smaller screens and preserve chart/table readability\n+\n+### Component Principles\n+- Navigation: dark vertical sidebar with icons and compact labels\n+- Filters: aligned, compact controls with equal heights\n+- KPI cards: color-block cards with large values and short captions\n+- Tables: minimal borders, readable headers, strong column rhythm\n+- Charts: restrained grid, one dominant accent color, simple line or area presentation\n+- Actions: icon-led row actions and clear primary buttons\n+\n+### Motion and Interaction\n+- Use subtle transitions only, around 150-250ms\n+- Animate state changes like hover, selected, filtered, and expanded\n+- Avoid decorative motion that does not improve clarity\n+\n+### Accessibility Baseline\n+- Keep contrast readable for text and controls\n+- Preserve visible focus states\n+- Avoid color-only meaning\n+- Use explicit labels and clear affordances\n+\n+## Design Tokens\n+Use these tokens as the default implementation target:\n+\n+```css\n+:root {\n+  --ux-nav-bg: #142638;\n+  --ux-app-bg: #F4F7FB;\n+  --ux-surface: #FFFFFF;\n+  --ux-border: #E6ECF2;\n+  --ux-accent: #27B1E6;\n+\n+  --ux-kpi-yellow: #F0C419;\n+  --ux-kpi-violet: #6E62C6;\n+  --ux-kpi-green: #22B455;\n+  --ux-kpi-sky: #2EA9DF;\n+\n+  --ux-text-heading: #1D2A3A;\n+  --ux-text-body: #4A5A6A;\n+  --ux-text-muted: #7E8B98;\n+  --ux-text-on-dark: #DCE6F2;\n+\n+  --ux-font-sans: \"Manrope\", \"Plus Jakarta Sans\", \"Segoe UI\", sans-serif;\n+  --ux-font-size-xs: 0.75rem;\n+  --ux-font-size-sm: 0.875rem;\n+  --ux-font-size-md: 1rem;\n+  --ux-font-size-lg: 1.25rem;\n+  --ux-font-size-xl: 1.75rem;\n+  --ux-font-weight-regular: 400;\n+  --ux-font-weight-medium: 500;\n+  --ux-font-weight-semibold: 600;\n+  --ux-font-weight-bold: 700;\n+\n+  --ux-space-1: 0.25rem;\n+  --ux-space-2: 0.5rem;\n+  --ux-space-3: 0.75rem;\n+  --ux-space-4: 1rem;\n+  --ux-space-5: 1.5rem;\n+  --ux-space-6: 2rem;\n+\n+  --ux-radius-sm: 0.375rem;\n+  --ux-radius-md: 0.625rem;\n+  --ux-radius-lg: 0.875rem;\n+  --ux-shadow-soft: 0 2px 8px rgba(20, 38, 56, 0.08);\n+  --ux-shadow-card: 0 6px 18px rgba(20, 38, 56, 0.10);\n+\n+  --ux-motion-fast: 150ms;\n+  --ux-motion-base: 220ms;\n+  --ux-ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1);\n+}\n+```\n+\n+## Recommended Workflow\n+1. Define the UX problem in one sentence.\n+2. Decide the main user goal and the primary screen state.\n+3. Choose the layout pattern and component structure.\n+4. Apply the workspace tokens and palette.\n+5. Validate responsiveness, accessibility, and visual uniqueness.\n+\n+## Output Format\n+When using this skill, prefer this output:\n+- UX diagnosis\n+- Visual direction\n+- Component and layout spec\n+- Implementation details\n+- Validation checklist\n+\n+## Example Prompts\n+- Design a non-default landing page for the support console using the workspace token palette.\n+- Improve the bookings list so it feels like a custom operations dashboard with breadcrumbs and detail links.\n+- Redesign the ticket detail screen with better hierarchy, spacing, and actions.\n+- Propose a responsive navigation system that does not look like Bootstrap.\n+\n+## Notes\n+- This skill is a UX playbook, not a backend or data skill.\n+- For implementation, keep the UI unique and intentional rather than generic or template-like.\n+- If a UX Specialist subagent exists in the workspace, use it for actual UX implementation tasks and keep this skill aligned with its design system.\n*** End Patch"},"tool_use_id":"call_3IJzOXZ6zbG9bD2P9OjPPU0l__vscode-1778141131562","cwd":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos"}

one location has many `Vehicles` through `Vehicle.CurrentLocation`\n  - one location is referenced by many `Bookings` as pickup location\n  - one location is referenced by many `Bookings` as planned dropoff location\n  - one location is referenced by many `SupportTickets` as requested dropoff location\n\n### Vehicle\n- `Id`\n- `PlateNumber`\n- `Brand`\n- `Model`\n- `ProductionYear`\n- `Category`\n- `DailyRate`\n- `CurrentMileage`\n- `IsAvailable`\n- `CurrentLocationId`\n- Relationship: one vehicle belongs to one `Location`, and one vehicle has many `Bookings`\n\n### Booking\n- `Id`\n- `ReservationCode`\n- `CustomerId`\n- `VehicleId`\n- `PickupLocationId`\n- `PlannedDropoffLocationId`\n- `PickupAt`\n- `PlannedDropoffAt`\n- `ActualDropoffAt`\n- `TotalPrice`\n- `Status`\n- Relationship: one booking belongs to one `Customer`, one `Vehicle`, one pickup `Location`, one planned dropoff `Location`, and has many `SupportTickets`\n\n### SupportAgent\n- `Id`\n- `FullName`\n- `Email`\n- `TeamName`\n- `ShiftStart`\n- `ShiftEnd`\n- `IsOnDuty`\n- Relationship: many agents can be assigned to many `SupportTickets`\n\n### SupportTicket\n- `Id`\n- `TicketNumber`\n- `BookingId`\n- `RequestedDropoffLocationId`\n- `Description`\n- `CreatedAt`\n- `ResolvedAt`\n- `Priority`\n- `Status`\n- Relationships:\n  - one ticket belongs to one `Booking`\n  - one ticket belongs to one requested dropoff `Location`\n  - many tickets can be assigned to many `SupportAgent` records\n\n## Relationship Summary\n- `Customer 1-N Booking`\n- `Vehicle 1-N Booking`\n- `Location 1-N Vehicle`\n- `Location 1-N Booking` as pickup location\n- `Location 1-N Booking` as planned dropoff location\n- `Location 1-N SupportTicket` as requested dropoff location\n- `Booking 1-N SupportTicket`\n- `SupportAgent N-N SupportTicket`\n"},"tool_use_id":"call_xMEKtubyZAFxwkwnafObv5fU__vscode-1778159459066","cwd":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos"}

{"timestamp":"2026-05-07T15:24:46.397Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"62fa44ff-e067-40fb-8542-1a869421830b","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\rober\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\6342806b270b2249f8fb3d4eb51eddaa\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\62fa44ff-e067-40fb-8542-1a869421830b.jsonl","tool_name":"create_file","tool_input":{"filePath":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos\\.github\\skills\\ef\\SKILL.md","content":"---\nname: ef\ndescription: \"Use when working with Entity Framework Core in this workspace: DbContext, DbSet, entity annotations, relationships, migrations, seeding, Include queries, and applying database updates.\"\n---\n\n# Entity Framework Skill\n\nThis skill covers the EF Core workflow for the PrviLabos solution.\n\nUse it when the task involves:\n- Adding or adjusting EF-ready model annotations\n- Configuring `DbContext` or `DbSet` members\n- Working with relationships, eager loading, or query shaping\n- Creating, removing, or updating migrations\n- Applying migrations to the database\n- Seeding data through `OnModelCreating`\n\n## Default Workflow\n1. Confirm the active `DbContext` and startup project.\n2. Check whether the model project stays POCO-only or needs EF configuration in `DbContext`.\n3. Prefer fluent configuration for provider-specific concerns such as decimal precision.\n4. Generate or update the migration from the DAL project.\n5. Apply the migration with `dotnet ef database update`.\n6. Rebuild the solution and verify the result.\n\n## Migration Commands\nUse the DAL project as the migration target and the web project as the startup project.\n\n```powershell\ncd PrviLabos.DAL\n& \"C:\\Program Files\\dotnet\\dotnet.exe\" ef migrations add Initial --startup-project ..\\PrviLabos --context PrviLabosDbContext\n& \"C:\\Program Files\\dotnet\\dotnet.exe\" ef database update --startup-project ..\\PrviLabos --context PrviLabosDbContext\n```\n\n## Model Rules\n- Keep domain entities simple and EF-friendly.\n- Use `Id` as the primary key.\n- Use `ICollection<T>` for collection navigations.\n- Use foreign key properties for explicit relationships.\n- Prefer `HasPrecision(18, 2)` in `OnModelCreating` for money values.\n\n## Query Rules\n- Use `Include` and `ThenInclude` when views need related data.\n- Use `AsNoTracking()` for read-only dashboard queries when tracking is unnecessary.\n- Avoid unsupported expression-tree constructs in EF queries.\n\n## Validation\n- Run `dotnet build` after EF changes.\n- Inspect generated migration files before applying them.\n- If `dotnet ef` complains about startup references, add `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design` to the startup project and rebuild.\n"},"tool_use_id":"call_I4NBcz8TWzdOGJxamt2eU85i__vscode-1778159459074","cwd":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos"}

Category = vehicle.Category,\n+            DailyRate = vehicle.DailyRate,\n+            CurrentMileage = vehicle.CurrentMileage,\n+            IsAvailable = vehicle.IsAvailable,\n+ location).ToList();\n+            location.SupportTickets = tickets.Where(ticket => ticket.RequestedDropoffLocation == location).ToList();\n+        }\n+\n+        foreach (var customer in customers)\n+        {\n+            customer.Bookings = bookings.Where(booking => booking.Customer == customer).ToList();\n+        }\n+\n+        foreach (var vehicle in vehicles)\n+        {\n+            vehicle.Bookings = bookings.Where(booking => booking.Vehicle == vehicle).ToList();\n+        }\n+\n+        foreach (var agent in agents)\n+        {\n+            agent.AssignedTickets = tickets.Where(ticket => ticket.AssignedAgents.Contains(agent)).ToList();\n+        }\n+\n+        context.Locations.AddRange(locations);\n+        context.Customers.AddRange(customers);\n+        context.Agents.AddRange(agents);\n+        context.Vehicles.AddRange(vehicles);\n+        context.Bookings.AddRange(bookings);\n+        context.Tickets.AddRange(tickets);\n \n         await `DriverLicenseNumber`\n-- `DateOfBirth`\n-- `RegisteredAt`\n-- Relationship: one customer has many `Bookings`\n-\n-### Location\n-- `Id`\n-- `Name`\n-- `City`\n-- `Address`\n-- `ContactPhone`\n-- `OpenAt`\n-- `CloseAt`\n-- `ParkingCapacity`\n-- Relationships:\n-  - one location has many `Vehicles` through `Vehicle.CurrentLocation`\n-  - one location is referenced by many `Bookings` as pickup location\n-  - one location is referenced by many `Bookings` as planned dropoff location\n-  - one location is referenced by many `SupportTickets` as requested dropoff location\n-\n-### Vehicle\n-- `Id`\n-- `PlateNumber`\n-- `Brand`\n-- `Model`\n-- `ProductionYear`\n-- `Category`\n-- `DailyRate`\n-- `CurrentMileage`\n-- `IsAvailable`\n-- `CurrentLocationId`\n-- Relationship: one vehicle belongs to one `Location`, and one vehicle has many `Bookings`\n-\n-### Booking\n-- `Id`\n-- `ReservationCode`\n-- `CustomerId`\n-- `VehicleId`\n-- `PickupLocationId`\n-- `PlannedDropoffLocationId`\n-- `PickupAt`\n-- `PlannedDropoffAt`\n-- `ActualDropoffAt`\n-- `TotalPrice`\n-- `Status`\n-- Relationship: one booking belongs to one `Customer`, one `Vehicle`, one pickup `Location`, one planned dropoff `Location`, and has many `SupportTickets`\n-\n-### SupportAgent\n-- `Id`\n-- `FullName`\n-- `Email`\n-- `TeamName`\n-- `ShiftStart`\n-- `ShiftEnd`\n-- `IsOnDuty`\n-- Relationship: many agents can be assigned to many `SupportTickets`\n-\n-### SupportTicket\n-- `Id`\n-- `TicketNumber`\n-- `BookingId`\n-- `RequestedDropoffLocationId`\n-- `Description`\n-- `CreatedAt`\n-- `ResolvedAt`\n-- `Priority`\n-- `Status`\n-- Relationships:\n-  - one ticket belongs to one `Booking`\n-  - one ticket belongs to one requested dropoff `Location`\n-  - many tickets can be assigned to many `SupportAgent` records\n-\n-## Relationship Summary\n-- `Customer 1-N Booking`\n-- `Vehicle 1-N Booking`\n-- `Location 1-N Vehicle`\n-- `Location 1-N Booking` as pickup location\n-- `Location 1-N Booking` as planned dropoff location\n-- `Location 1-N SupportTicket` as requested dropoff location\n-- `Booking 1-N SupportTicket`\n-- `SupportAgent N-N SupportTicket`\n+# Semantic Model\n+\n+Sa++etak modela i relacija za EF Core bazu u projektu `PrviLabos`.\n+\n+## Entiteti i tablice\n+\n+### Customer -> `Customers`\n+- `Id`\n+- `FirstName`\n+- `LastName`\n+- `Email`\n+- `PhoneNumber`\n+- `DriverLicenseNumber`\n+- `DateOfBirth`\n+- `RegisteredAt`\n+\n+### Location -> `Locations`\n+- `Id`\n+- `Name`\n+- `City`\n+- `Address`\n+- `ContactPhone`\n+- `OpenAt`\n+- `CloseAt`\n+- `ParkingCapacity`\n+\n+### Vehicle -> `Vehicles`\n+- `Id`\n+- `PlateNumber`\n+- `Brand`\n+- `Model`\n+- `ProductionYear`\n+- `Category`\n+- `DailyRate`\n+- `CurrentMileage`\n+- `IsAvailable`\n+- `CurrentLocationId`\n+\n+### Booking -> `Bookings`\n+- `Id`\n+- `ReservationCode`\n+- `CustomerId`\n+- `VehicleId`\n+- `PickupLocationId`\n+- `PlannedDropoffLocationId`\n+- `PickupAt`\n+- `PlannedDropoffAt`\n+- `ActualDropoffAt`\n+- `TotalPrice`\n+- `Status`\n+\n+### SupportAgent -> `Agents`\n+- `Id`\n+- `FullName`\n+- `Email`\n+- `TeamName`\n+- `ShiftStart`\n+- `ShiftEnd`\n+- `IsOnDuty`\n+\n+### SupportTicket -> `Tickets`\n+- `Id`\n+- `TicketNumber`\n+- `BookingId`\n+- `RequestedDropoffLocationId`\n+- `Description`\n+- `CreatedAt`\n+- `ResolvedAt`\n+- `Priority`\n+- `Status`\n+\n+### Vrijednosni tipovi (enumovi)\n+- `VehicleCategory`\n+- `BookingStatus`\n+- `TicketPriority`\n+- `TicketStatus`\n+\n+## Veze me-ću tablicama\n+\n+- `Customer` 1:N `Booking` preko `Booking.CustomerId`\n+- `Vehicle` 1:N `Booking` preko `Booking.VehicleId`\n+- `Location` 1:N `Vehicle` preko `Vehicle.CurrentLocationId`\n+- `Location` 1:N `Booking` kao pickup lokacija preko `Booking.PickupLocationId`\n+- `Location` 1:N `Booking` kao planned dropoff lokacija preko `Booking.PlannedDropoffLocationId`\n+- `Booking` 1:N `SupportTicket` preko `SupportTicket.BookingId`\n+- `Location` 1:N `SupportTicket` kao requested dropoff lokacija preko `SupportTicket.RequestedDropoffLocationId`\n+- `SupportAgent` N:N `SupportTicket` preko implicitne spojne tablice `SupportAgentSupportTicket`\n+\n+## Kratki pregled\n+\n+- Glavne poslovne cjeline su korisnici, vozila, lokacije, rezervacije i podr+íka.\n+- `Booking` povezuje korisnika, vozilo i dvije lokacije.\n+- `SupportTicket` je vezan uz rezervaciju i lokaciju, a mo++e biti dodijeljen vi+íe agenata.\n*** End
